SddSetup.Purpose: Creates or updates specs/README.md — Vision and Scope Graph. Sole owner of the Portal. Idempotent.
SddSetup.Triggers: new project · add scope · change project vision · register scope after discovery
SddSetup.SkipWhen: designing a specific scope · module decomposition · task scaffolding

SddDiscovery.Purpose: Scope-level discovery session. Creates or evolves specs/[scope]/[scope].spec.md. Branches by scope-type: infrastructure, contracts, library, product.
SddDiscovery.Triggers: design scope · raw idea for service/SDK/CLI/app · bootstrap infra tooling · refine or pivot existing scope
SddDiscovery.SkipWhen: approved spec exists and operator wants module decomposition · bug fix or local refactor · project-level vision change

SddModuleDecomposition.Purpose: Decomposes a product or library scope into module specs with closed-world entity inventory, public surfaces, DbC contracts (Ports / Adapters / Services).
SddModuleDecomposition.Triggers: discovery complete · split into modules · build module map · list all entities · produce DbC · Ports and Adapters
SddModuleDecomposition.SkipWhen: scope spec missing or not approved · discovery still open · scope-type is infrastructure or contracts · tasks already generated

SddTaskScaffolding.Purpose: Builds DAG of compact task tickets from scope graph. Each ticket includes BDD, Effective Rules, Verification, Execution Log template.
SddTaskScaffolding.Triggers: all scope specs ready · break into tasks · build DAG · produce execution plan · scaffold tickets
SddTaskScaffolding.SkipWhen: module specs lack contracts · discovery or module-decomp not closed · tasks already generated
SddTaskScaffolding.Preconditions: specs/README.md Scope Graph ✅; coding rule files exist for each language

SddTaskExecution.Purpose: Execute one task ticket end-to-end: code + tests + Execution Log.
SddTaskExecution.SkipWhen: planning still open · no ticket selected
SddTaskExecution.Preconditions: tasks/README.md exists; ≥1 [ ] TODO ticket with deps [x]DONE

SddAudit.Purpose: Post-execution verification. Compares spec ↔ ticket ↔ code. Detects drift, gaps, rules violations. Output: ephemeral findings routed to spec/ticket.
SddAudit.Triggers: audit · drift detection · post-implementation review · verify implementation · end of epic before merge
SddAudit.SkipWhen: no executed tasks · Execution Log empty · no spec to compare
SddAudit.Preconditions: spec exists; ticket with non-empty Execution Log; code committed

Rule.Purpose: Writing code in the chosen language: typing, DbC, patterns, anti-patterns.
Rule.Triggers: Target Files include source code files (not config)
Rule.SkipWhen: Config-only task; infra-setup task without code files
Rule.ActivationHint: Before editing or creating any source code file

Rule.Triggers: Target Test Files present AND stack uses Vitest
Rule.SkipWhen: Stack uses a different test runner; no test files in scope
Rule.ActivationHint: Before writing or modifying test files

Rule.Triggers: Target Test Files present AND stack uses node:test
Rule.SkipWhen: Stack uses a different test runner; no test files in scope
Rule.ActivationHint: Before writing or modifying test files

Rule.Purpose: Configuring a linter: severity policy, autofix, config format, formatter integration.
Rule.Triggers: Task configures linter · installs linter · sets lint rules
Rule.SkipWhen: Coding/testing task that only RUNS lint, does not configure it
Rule.ActivationHint: Before writing linter config or modifying lint rules

Rule.Purpose: Configuring VCS: ignore baseline, branches, commits, hooks, secrets discipline.
Rule.Triggers: Task bootstraps repo · configures VCS ignore file · sets up hooks · commit convention
Rule.SkipWhen: Task only commits code changes (does not configure VCS itself)
Rule.ActivationHint: Before writing VCS ignore file or configuring hooks

Rule.Purpose: Runtime + package manager setup: version pinning, single package manager, module system, mandatory check script.
Rule.Triggers: Task writes package manager manifest · runtime version file · sets engines · chooses package manager
Rule.SkipWhen: Task does not touch package manager manifest or runtime config
Rule.ActivationHint: Before writing package manager manifest or runtime version file
